howItWorks.intro: This life insurance needs calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: DIME Method
rules.explanation: Sum of income replacement, debts, and education funding needs.

howItWorks.outro: Insurance rates depend on many individual factors. Get personalized quotes from licensed providers.

faq.question: How much life insurance do I need?
faq.answer: The DIME method: Debt + Income replacement + Mortgage + Education costs.

faq.question: Term vs whole life?
faq.answer: Term is cheaper and covers most needs. Whole life includes investment but costs 5-10x more.

faq.question: Is the Life Insurance Needs Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard insurance planning formulas trusted by individuals and families. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What annual income should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate annual income value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Life Insurance Needs Calculator?
sections.body: The Life Insurance Needs Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the insurance planning space. By entering your annual income, years to replace income, total debts, you get instant results including total coverage needed, coverage gap. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total coverage needed right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In insurance planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to choose the right coverage with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your annual income and need to find the right total coverage needed. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Annual Income ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your annual income — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: years to replace income, total debts, children's education, existing coverage. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your total coverage needed, coverage gap. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your annual income before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both total coverage needed and coverage gap — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to choose the right coverage.
If you're unsure about your existing coverage, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
